questions.question: Why do `newValue` and `oldValue` point to the same object when deep-watching a reactive object?
options: Vue clones the object but the clone is shallow
Vue doesn't clone the object before mutation, so both references reflect the mutated state
Deep watching is only supported for arrays, not plain objects
Both values are proxied through the same Proxy handler

questions.question: When do `newValue` and `oldValue` actually differ when watching an object?
options: When using `{ deep: true }` with a large number of nested properties
When the entire object is replaced (new reference), not just mutated
They always differ when `immediate: true` is set

questions.question: Which technique captures a snapshot of an object so old/new values differ in the watch callback?
options: Pass `{ clone: true }` to the watch options
Return a shallow clone `() => ({ ...state.user })` from the getter
Use `shallowRef` instead of `reactive` for the object
